org.key_project.util.collection

Interface ImmutableSet<T>

  • All Superinterfaces:
    java.lang.Iterable<T>, java.io.Serializable
    All Known Implementing Classes:
    DefaultImmutableSet

    public interface ImmutableSet<T>
extends java.lang.Iterable<T>, java.io.Serializable
    interface implemented by non-destructive Sets. CONVENTION: Each SetOf implementation has to offer a public static final variable .nil()

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Static Methods Instance Methods Abstract Methods Default Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      default ImmutableSet<T> add(java.lang.Iterable<T> seq) 
      ImmutableSet<T> add(T element)
      Adds an element
      ImmutableSet<T> addUnique(T element)
      adds an element, barfs if the element is already present
      static <T> java.util.stream.Collector<T,java.util.Set<T>,ImmutableSet<T>> collector()
      Returns a Collector that accumulates the input elements into a new ImmutableSet.
      boolean contains(T obj) 
      static <T> ImmutableSet<T> empty() 
      boolean equals(java.lang.Object o) 
      boolean exists(java.util.function.Predicate<T> predicate)
      return true if predicate is fullfilled for at least one element
      static <T> ImmutableSet<T> fromCollection(java.util.Collection<? extends T> seq) 
      static <T> ImmutableSet<T> fromSet(java.util.Set<T> set)
      Creates an ImmutableSet from a Set.

      ImmutableSet<T> intersect(ImmutableSet<? extends T> set) 
      boolean isEmpty()

      Builds a single set with the given obj.

      java.util.stream.Stream<T> stream() 
      boolean subset(ImmutableSet<T> s) 
      <S> S[] toArray(S[] array)
      Convert the set to a Java array

      ImmutableSet<T> union(ImmutableSet<? extends T> set)

        forEach, spliterator

    • Method Detail

      • collector

        static <T> java.util.stream.Collector<T,java.util.Set<T>,ImmutableSet<T>> collector()
        Returns a Collector that accumulates the input elements into a new ImmutableSet.
        Returns:
        a Collector that accumulates the input elements into a new ImmutableSet.

      • fromSet

        static <T> ImmutableSet<T> fromSet(java.util.Set<T> set)
        Creates an ImmutableSet from a Set.
        Parameters:
        set - a Set.
        Returns:
        an ImmutableSet containing the same elements as the specified set.

      • singleton

        static <T> ImmutableSet<T> singleton(T obj)
        Builds a single set with the given obj.

      • empty

        static <T> ImmutableSet<T> empty()

      • fromCollection

        static <T> ImmutableSet<T> fromCollection(@Nonnull
                                          java.util.Collection<? extends T> seq)

      • toSet

        java.util.Set<T> toSet()
        Returns:
        a Set containing the same elements as this ImmutableSet

      • add

        ImmutableSet<T> add(T element)
        Adds an element
        Returns:
        a set containing all elements of this one and the specified element.

      • union

        ImmutableSet<T> union(ImmutableSet<? extends T> set)
        Returns:
        union of this set with set

      • intersect

        ImmutableSet<T> intersect(ImmutableSet<? extends T> set)
        Returns:
        intersection of this set with set

      • iterator

        java.util.Iterator<T> iterator()
        Specified by:
        iterator in interface java.lang.Iterable<T>
        Returns:
        Iterator of the set

      • stream

        java.util.stream.Stream<T> stream()
        Returns:
        Stream of the set

      • exists

        boolean exists(java.util.function.Predicate<T> predicate)
        return true if predicate is fullfilled for at least one element
        Parameters:
        predicate - the predicate
        Returns:
        true if predicate is fullfilled for at least one element

      • contains

        boolean contains(T obj)
        Returns:
        true iff obj in set

      • subset

        boolean subset(ImmutableSet<T> s)
        Returns:
        true iff this set is subset of set s

      • size

        int size()
        Returns:
        int the cardinality of the set

      • isEmpty

        boolean isEmpty()
        Returns:
        true iff the set is empty

      • remove

        ImmutableSet<T> remove(T element)
        Returns:
        set without element

      • equals

        boolean equals(java.lang.Object o)
        Overrides:
        equals in class java.lang.Object
        Returns:
        true iff the this set is subset of o and vice versa.

      • hashCode

        int hashCode()
        Overrides:
        hashCode in class java.lang.Object

      • addUnique

        ImmutableSet<T> addUnique(T element)
                   throws NotUniqueException
        adds an element, barfs if the element is already present
        Parameters:
        element - of type that has to be added to this set
        Throws:
        NotUniqueException - if the element is already present

        <S> S[] toArray(S[] array)
        Convert the set to a Java array
